channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: An armed man kills at least 10 people, including 2 children, in a shooting rampage in Montenegro
item.description: PODGORICA, Montenegro (AP) — At least 10 people, including two children, were killed and four others were seriously wounded on Wednesday in a shooting rampage that followed a bar brawl in a western Montenegrin city, officials said. The shooter was on the run.
item.dc:creator: PREDRAG MILIC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Protesters in Syria demand justice for disappeared activists and accountability from all factions
item.description: DOUMA, Syria (AP) — Protesters in Syria held a sit-in Wednesday demanding justice for four activists who were forcibly disappeared in 2013 and whose fate remains one of the most haunting mysteries of the country's 13-year civil war. On Dec.
item.dc:creator: OMAR ALBAM and SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli strikes kill 12 in Gaza, including children, as war grinds into the new year
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli strikes killed at least 12 Palestinians in the Gaza Strip, mostly women and children, officials said Wednesday, as the nearly 15-month war ground on into the new year.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Power is restored to nearly all of Puerto Rico after a major blackout
item.description: BAYAMÓN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Power was restored to nearly all electrical customers across Puerto Rico on Wednesday after a sweeping blackout plunged the U.S. territory into darkness on New Year’s Eve.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA PÉREZ PINTADO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Pope calls for commitment to protect life as he doubles down on abortion in New Year's Day message
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis ushered in the New Year with a renewed appeal for the faithful to reject abortion, calling for a “firm commitment” to protect and respect life from conception to natural death. Francis, 88, celebrated a New Year’s Day Mass in St.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine halts transit of Russian gas to Europe after a prewar deal expired
item.description: Ukraine has halted Russian gas supplies to European customers through its pipeline network, almost three years into Moscow’s all-out invasion of its neighbor.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The world welcomes 2025 with light shows, embraces and ice plunges
item.description: The New Year’s Eve ball dropped in soggy Times Square, where thousands of revelers stuck it out in heavy rain to celebrate.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, CEDAR ATTANASIO and CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden speaks to Puerto Rico governor, Energy secretary about power outages
item.description: President Biden said he spoke with the governor of Puerto Rico and the Energy secretary after a blackout hit nearly all of the island early Tuesday morning. During their talk, Biden offered Puerto Rico Gov.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Israel kills Hamas commander who led heinous Oct. 7 attack on Kibbutz Nir Oz killed in drone attack: IDF
item.description: The Israel Defense Force announced that they had successfully killed one of the terrorist leaders who was responsible for the Oct. 7 attack on Israel in a targeted attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump, Putin advisers trade barbs heading into new year
item.description: The year is ending with a pair of sharp messages between top advisers to Russian leader Vladimir Putin and President-elect Trump. Trump’s choice for special envoy to Ukraine and Russia, retired Lt. Gen. Keith Kellogg, slammed a Russian missile and drone attack from last week.
item.dc:creator: Colin Meyn, The Hill

item.title: A new year dawns on a Middle East torn by conflict and change
item.description: DAMASCUS (AP) — In Damascus, the streets were buzzing with excitement Tuesday as Syrians welcomed in a new year that seemed to many to bring a promise of a brighter future after the unexpected fall of Bashar Assad’s government weeks earlier.
item.dc:creator: GHAITH ALSAYED, WAFAA SHURAFA and LUJAIN JO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Berlin police say they have detained a man who attacked 2 people with a knife
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Berlin police said they detained a man Tuesday who attacked and injured two people in the city's Charlottenburg neighborhood, further jolting Germany days after a deadly Christmas market attack elsewhere in the country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lemon calls Trump supporters 'f‑‑‑ing idiots' amid H-1B visa infighting
item.description: Former CNN host Don Lemon poked fun at the Republican Party's infighting over H-1B visas — a key program to attract international talent that’s been criticized as too complicated and susceptible to abuse — calling supporters of President-elect Trump "dumb f‑‑‑ing idiots.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Montenegro extradites cryptocurrency mogul Do Kwon to the United States
item.description: PODGORICA, Montenegro (AP) — Montenegro on Tuesday extradited a South Korean mogul known as “the cryptocurrency king” to the United States, following a decision of its justice ministry earlier this month to accept a U.S.
item.dc:creator: PREDRAG MILIC, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Zimbabwe's new currency woes hit traditional stores while illegal night bazaars flourish
item.description: HARARE, Zimbabwe (AP) — Batsirai Pabwe picked detergents, toothpaste, snacks and some pasta among several grocery items spread on the tarmac of a car parking space — an unorthodox night bazaar lit by cellphone flashlights and fluorescent lamps in Zimbabwe’s capital, Harare.
item.dc:creator: FARAI MUTSAKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea to inspect Boeing aircraft as it struggles to find cause of plane crash that killed 179
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean officials said Monday they will conduct safety inspections of all Boeing 737-800 aircraft operated by the country's airlines, as they struggle to determine what caused a plane crash that killed 179 people a day earlier.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia, Ukraine swap more than 300 prisoners
item.description: Russia and Ukraine announced Monday a swap of more than 300 prisoners of war. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky announced his team secured the return of 189 Ukrainians, while Russia announced the return of 150 service members.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Uncovering the atrocities of the Assad regime at its 'death factory' on the hill
item.description: Sednaya prison was the site where thousands of Syrians were tortured and killed under Assad’s regime. Investigators have uncovered mass graves, and are calling for justice.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US sends Ukraine final Biden-approved weapons, financial package
item.description: The Biden administration announced Monday its final weapons and aid package to Ukraine before President-elect Trump is set to take the reins in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Finnish investigators find an anchor drag mark on the Baltic seabed after suspicious cable damage
item.description: HELSINKI, Finland (AP) — Finnish investigators probing the damage to a Baltic Sea power cable and several data cables said they found an anchor drag mark on the seabed, apparently from a Russia-linked vessel that has already been seized.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Syria rebel leader says new elections could take up to 4 years
item.description: Ahmad al-Sharaa, the leader of the new governing authority in Syria, said in a Sunday interview that it could take up to four years to hold elections in the country.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: South Korea plane's final moments captured on video before hitting concrete barrier, triggering explosion
item.description: A video released Sunday shows the exact moment a plane skidded off a runway in South Korea before crashing into a concrete barrier, causing an explosion.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China directs largest military build-up since 1930s Nazi Germany, expert warns, citing Pentagon report
item.description: Defense Department report reveals China's rapidly growing weapons arsenal and "psychological warfare" operation ahead of new Trump presidency.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea vows 'toughest' US policy in vague announcement
item.description: North Korean Dictator Kim Jong Un's regime vowed to adopt its "toughest" policies against the U.S. during its annual meeting last week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu to undergo major surgery after UTI diagnosis
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu will undergo prostate removal surgery on Sunday amid multiple conflicts across the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Plane skids off runway in South Korea, killing at least 177
item.description: A passenger plane burst into flames Sunday after it skidded off a runway at a South Korean airport and slammed into a concrete fence when its front landing gear apparently failed to deploy.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-Jin Kim, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Eyewitnesses to South Korea plane crash recount sparks in engine, bird strike before collision
item.description: Witnesses to a deadly plane crash in South Korea said they observed flames in the engine of the aircraft, heard explosions and noticed birds colliding with the plane.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China sanctions 7 companies over US military assistance to Taiwan
item.description: The sanctions also come in response to the recent approval of the U.S. government's annual defense spending bill, which a Chinese Foreign Ministry statement said “includes multiple negative sections on China.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US teacher jailed in Russia is formally designated as wrongfully detained
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An American schoolteacher arrested in Russia on drug charges more than three years ago has been designated by the U.S. government as wrongfully detained, the State Department said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Mexico tests app for migrants to send alert if they're about to be detained in US
item.description: U.S. authorities are obliged to give notice to home-country consulates when a foreign citizen is detained. Mexico says it has beefed up consular staff and legal aid to help migrants in the legal process related to deportation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Azerbaijani airliner crashes in Kazakhstan, killing 38 with 29 survivors, officials say
item.description: An Azerbaijani airliner with 67 people onboard crashed Wednesday near the Kazakhstani city of Aktau, killing 38 people and leaving 29 survivors, a Kazakh official said.
item.dc:creator: KATIE MARIE DAVIES and DASHA LITVINOVA, Associated Press, NewsNation
